items.title: USDA finalizes tougher SNAP retailer stocking standards (compliance starts Nov. 4, 2026)
items.summary: USDA published a final rule updating SNAP retailer stocking standards that takes effect Nov. 4, 2026. SNAP-authorized retailers must stock at least 7 varieties in each of 4 staple food categories (dairy, vegetables or fruits, grains, and protein) and include perishable varieties in at least 3 of the 4 categories. Stores that do not comply can be withdrawn from SNAP and may generally reapply after 6 months.

items.title: CMS guidance: new limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued guidance to prepare states for a statutory change limiting when states can claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or U.S. nationals or who fall into specific noncitizen categories identified in law. CMS said states must implement the change beginning Oct. 1, 2026.

items.title: IRS: 2026 inflation adjustments raise maximum Earned Income Tax Credit to $8,231 (3+ children)
items.summary: The IRS released tax year 2026 inflation adjustments, including updated Earned Income Tax Credit amounts. The maximum EITC for tax year 2026 is $8,231 for filers with three or more qualifying children (up from $8,046 for tax year 2025).

items.title: SSI: 2026 maximum federal payment amounts set at $994 (individual) and $1,491 (eligible couple)
items.summary: SSA published the 2026 maximum federal SSI payment amounts. For 2026, the monthly maximum is $994 for an eligible individual and $1,491 for an eligible couple (with $498 for an essential person).

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit at $26 (children), $48 (pregnant/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding)
items.summary: USDA issued WIC guidance setting the monthly cash-value benefit (CVB) amounts for FY 2026 (Oct. 1, 2025–Sept. 30, 2026). The FY 2026 monthly CVB amounts are $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or partially breastfeeding participants.

items.title: FAFSA deadline reminder: 2025–26 FAFSA federal deadline is June 30, 2026
items.summary: USA.gov notes that students seeking federal student aid for the 2025–26 school year must submit the FAFSA by the federal deadline of June 30, 2026. For the 2026–27 school year, the federal FAFSA deadline is June 30, 2027.

items.title: USDA announces nutrition-assistance reorganization and new hub locations (SNAP operations moving to Indianapolis)
items.summary: USDA announced plans to introduce a new Food and Nutrition Administration and relocate leadership and staff from Washington, D.C. to multiple hubs and compliance offices around the country. USDA said SNAP would be relocated to Indianapolis, with other nutrition program functions moving to hubs including Dallas, Kansas City, Raleigh, and Denver.
